economic_notes: First Nation has a 1/7 ownership interest in South Beach Group of Partnerships (casino and hotel operations). Beneficial owner of 12.5% of Shawano Wapunong Investments Corp. Invested $1 in Southern Chiefs Economic Development Corporation (2.94% interest). Pursuing legal claim against Government of Canada funded by demand loan up to $4,669,000.
notes: Fiscal year 2020 statements. Auditor issued Emphasis of Matter paragraph regarding demand loan payable of $3,290,235 taken to pursue legal claim against Government of Canada (max $4,669,000). No asset recorded for potential settlement proceeds. First Nation is not in compliance with CMHC replacement reserve requirements (underfunded by $224,464) and has not established required CMHC operating surplus reserve. Consolidated entities include Black River First Nation Housing Authority, O'Hanley Enterprises Ltd., Members In Need Inc., and Black River Development Corporation. South Beach Group of Partnerships accounted for by modified equity method. Due to Province of Manitoba $337,528 outstanding since 2011 with no loan agreement or repayment terms. Contaminated sites identified but no liability recorded. expenses_housing combines Housing ($1,084,262) and Subsidized Housing ($741,147). expenses_salaries_total taken from cash flow statement (cash paid to employees). total_government_transfers = ISC $11,144,954 + CMHC $367,098 = $11,512,052.
warnings: High ISC dependency (83.04%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
